content.problemStatement:
Naruto has recently joined the Ninja Academy for higher education, while filling the admission form he is required to choose minimum XXX subjects out of a list of NNN subjects. He is not very interested in studies but wants to get a good overall CPI. However, every subject has a different weightage/credit. The credit (denoted as CCCi) of a particular course is equal to the position in which it appears in the subject list, i.e, the first course has a credit=1, the second course has a credit=2, and so on.
Naruto knows his capabilities, and so he has come up with the scores (SSSi)  he can achieve in every course. He wants to maximise his CPI, which can be calculated as the weighted mean of all the subjects taken, denoted as
**Sum(Ci*Si)/Sum(Ci)** for all i s.t Naruto has taken up course i

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of every test case contains two integers, NNN and XXX
Next line of the test case contains NNN space separated integers Si, denoting the score he can get in every subject
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line the maximum cpi he can get after choosing the subjects. Your output will be considered correct if it has an absolute error of less than 10-6.

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000
1≤X≤N1 \leq X \leq N1≤X≤N
1≤S1 \leq S1≤Si≤10000   \leq 10000≤10000
###Sample Input:
1

5 2

50 40 30 20 10

###Sample Output:
43.3333333333

###Explanation:
He can achieve the max credit by picking the first two subjects, which produces a CPI of (50 * 1+40 * 2)/(1+2) = 43.333333333
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X, where N is the number of subjects and X is the minimum number of subjects to choose. The second line contains N space-separated integers Si, denoting the score Naruto can get in each subject.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the maximum CPI Naruto can get after choosing the subjects, with an absolute error of less than 10^-6.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Naruto chooses the first two subjects with scores 50 and 40, resulting in a CPI of (50 * 1 + 40 * 2) / (1 + 2) = 43.333333

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. We first calculate the CPI for each subject and then sort the subjects based on their CPI in descending order. We then choose the X subjects with the highest CPI to maximize the overall CPI.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) — due to sorting the subjects based on their CPI
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the subjects and their CPI

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, X = map(int, input().split())
    scores = list(map(int, input().split()))
    scores.sort(reverse=True)
    sum = 0
    weight = 0
    for i in range(X):
        sum += scores[i] * (i + 1)
        weight += (i + 1)
    print("{:.6f}".format(sum / weight))
